name: Freezer Tomato Sauce
recipeIngredient: 6 lb Ripe tomatoes; peeled and chopped (10 cups)
1 Cayenne chili pepper - seeded and finely chopped OR
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large kettle, cook onions, celery, and garlic in hot oil for about 5 minutes or until tender.
2. Add tomatoes, sugar, salt, black pepper, and chili pepper (red pepper). Bring to boil.
3. Reduce heat and simmer, uncovered, about 45 minutes or until desired consistency is reached, stirring occasionally.
4. Add oregano and thyme. Simmer, uncovered, for an additional 15 minutes.
5. Cool slightly.
6. Process the sauce in batches using a food processor or through a food mill to desired texture.
7. Transfer the sauce to a bowl set in ice water to cool quickly.
8. Fill freezer containers, seal tightly, label, and freeze. Makes 3 to 4 pints.
9. Serving size: 1/2 cup - 81 calories, 3 grams fat, no cholesterol, 2 grams protein, 14 grams carbohydrates, 4 grams fiber, 203 milligrams sodium (Source: Nancy Byal, AP, Sarasota Herald Tribune, 2/24/94)
